steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: We already know ∠A = ∠P = 52°. AA similarity requires just one more pair of equal angles. The right choice must supply information that produces a matching second angle pair in both triangles.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: gives sides, not angles — can't establish AA from side lengths alone. ✗
  • Choice B: gives only one additional angle in one triangle — insufficient to match angle pairs. ✗
  • Choice C — '∠B = 34° and ∠R = 94°': Triangle ABC gives ∠C = 180 − 52 − 34 = 94°; Triangle PQR gives ∠Q = 180 − 52 − 94 = 34°. All three pairs match: ∠A = ∠P, ∠B = ∠Q, ∠C = ∠R. ✓
  • Choice D: gives angles that don't produce matching pairs with the known 52°. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
